message:
feat(cli): improve SQLite health probes, doctor orphan detection, and MCP config URL repair

Refactor SQLite health probe logic by extracting the compatibility probe
error handling into a dedicated handle_sqlite_compatibility_probe_result
helper. The refactored flow now checks for live WAL/SHM sidecars early
and uses the compatibility probe as a fallback when the primary health
check fails on databases without active sidecars, preventing false
corruption reports on databases with orphaned foreign keys but no
structural damage.

Enhance doctor orphaned message recipient detection: replace the
row-based rowid query with a diagnostic query that reports exactly which
reference is missing (missing_message vs missing_agent flags), enabling
more targeted cleanup decisions.

Extend doctor check and doctor fix to detect and repair wrong HTTP URLs
in MCP config files, not just stdio-to-HTTP conversion. The check now
extracts the actual URL from each config, compares it against the
expected server URLs (supporting localhost/127.0.0.1 aliases and
multiple path variants), and warns when the port or path is stale.
Doctor fix rewrites mismatched HTTP URLs with the correct endpoint.

Update cursor.mcp.json and gemini.mcp.json port from 8772 to 8765 to
match the canonical server configuration.

Add tests:
- URL extraction from TOML MCP configs
- URL alias matching with port mismatch rejection
- HTTP URL port mismatch repair in existing TOML configs
- SQLite health acceptance with orphaned FKs but no WAL sidecars
- Doctor FK violation test using direct schema init instead of migration

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
